textByHash.fac83a4ed0161144276f8a098a59018528cff949: Welcome to the Library Historical Archive.
textByHash.a40772a2ac61ff438788c5499bd25ffdc9d7821d: Ah, poor girl. Her tale is such a tragic one. What would you like to know?
textByHash.27a349c1daae86718862184405cbbaa47ec06b34: You are correct that we have her diary. It's in the display case on the eastern wall. That said, I'm afraid I can't let you read it.
textByHash.b21b9d024522ec9c273fcefafcdc07cd56234b7e: The diary is here to be preserved. However, we do not feel it appropriate to let people read the private thoughts of a young girl.
textByHash.dd241fadb13f9f3ccf23fb54523268ea556e18a5: Is there anything else I can help you with?
textByHash.6584fddef6f407a663ead46182aaab7aeef55c6f: I think I have heard that name before. Who is she?
textByHash.3a99a9289af47058e6f8aad36326d0f2ff39d6d0: I see. We have very few records from before the founding of Kourend. That said, perhaps Imerominia could help you? As a horologist, such ancient topics may fall within her purview.
textByHash.79761cbc874cad7a2465bc4089f231fa41f920ca: Is there something else I can help you with?
textByHash.def98b99a252aae4db0461aa4fc59e510d48eb95: Well you've come to the right place. What would you like to know?
textByHash.ef6f008fe88e2f64113e14bfd3cb638e8fc06f2e: Ah yes, I know of that expedition. King Shayzien VII was one of Kourend's most notable rulers. According to the history books, his reign was defined by violence.
textByHash.8a097cb2049b0ed4bae932933758f3524bb1b991: The expedition to Quidamortem was part of his aggressive plans to expand Kourend's territory. Unfortunately, it was a failure. The entire party was killed.
textByHash.8b4a1c34d4ec7a7bc03988836bd39a4714ffc1bf: The King himself died a few years later and was succeeded by Xeric, a tyrannical mage who made the King seem meek in comparison. Though of course, many believe them to actually be the same person.
textByHash.f46440a62eaa93a15d0db8bb706273586ce1406c: But I digress. The key thing to note is that no more expeditions were ordered under King Shayzien VII's rule. Does that help you?
textByHash.27966e019ff20dd93c2ece3fcc6f81ac4251cddb: I'm afraid I can't help you there. All the evidence we have tells us that the expedition had no survivors.
textByHash.127b94325cd3fd53e46d8418520131a3682eb16c: It would seem that way. However, if you do want to learn more about King Shayzien VII, it might be worth talking to Pagida. He's recently been investigating the King's additions to the Tower of Magic.
textByHash.e7de14020092393a779998dffdd34a1bb2f058d4: Welcome to the Grand Library Historical Archive.
textByHash.d4a06b0fafffce8de2320eedfce2c9db5d8816a6: This is the Library Historical Archive. Here you'll find historical documents and texts from all eras of Great Kourend's history, dating all the way back to the first decade.
textByHash.13b9857adf3c59e132d6f95e2d42e69a530d7252: Are you looking for something in particular?
textByHash.eb868e8272bba7f142d08356e10c5858d1350411: Ah, Xeric. Our most infamous ruler... We do have extensive records regarding Xeric. You can examine some of the texts here or speak to Ektheme, our Xerician expert.
textByHash.1e90bbdd09a4e4fe5ab8724ce729659c0b2f5ac7: The Wintertodt, or the Winter beast as it was often known at the time, terrorised northern Kourend for five decades.
textByHash.7b967a1081dc76fda888f9ddda9ebf6049549b69: Logios is currently studying the Wintertodt Invasion, and we have various texts from that era.
textByHash.e46b863ce8de3003b29191112eafad4b80786342: Our first records of the mountain date back to the 36th decade where a Shayzien patrol discovered the mountain during a survey of the Kebos Lowlands.
textByHash.dd0d8300dc394dc2a314e7c37ea342e41de08fb6: King Shayzien VII was keen on conquering more land for Kourend and demanded his people explore suitable territories.
textByHash.1f2184113f0d9bbe009cb89cf7a2474785234071: The mountain became known as Mount Quidamortem after it was realised venturing towards the summit resulted in certain death.
textByHash.4fd6ab025bf2e0594c7846dd9d78c325ac99d71b: Gnosi is our expert on Mount Quidamortem, but I'm afraid she's currently on an expedition to the temple near the summit of the mountain. However, we do have some texts available detailing the great expedition.
textByHash.45bf59d1c4cfd0883c7e6328480e48c57295b3b3: My name is Istoria. I'm the chief historian and curator of the Library Historical Archive.
textByHash.d93036be6ca69d64730ed2f24fe30fbda7a50af6: I can teleport you back to the Grand Library. Would you like to leave now?
textByHash.e778c346c00fb7e7216fd8538a36a4474bc867b5: Ah yes, I know of that expedition. King Shayzien VII's reign was quite a violent one according to the history books. The expedition to Quidamortem was part of his aggressive plans to expand Kourend's territory.
textByHash.4d996e54cee46c57f1b052f22cb343ff3a32d117: Unfortunately, the expedition was a failure and the entire party was killed. The king himself died a few years later and was succeeded by the tyrant Xeric. No more expeditions were ordered under his rule.
textByHash.4a8c00bd88add4fcad99fa82f5c4c8df3c831ae0: I'm afraid I can't help you there, all the evidence we have tells us that the expedition had no survivors.
textByHash.79b11907e07139a5b94461f65fc0765c44799a1e: It would seem that way. However, if you do want to learn more about King Shayzien VII it might be worth talking to Pagida. He's recently been investigating the kings' additions to the Tower of Magic.
textByHash.6f86ff3104a4b3abb2ba53821f28e03458fd39fd: I have heard that name, though we have very few records from before the founding of Kourend. Perhaps Imerominia could help you? She is the Horologist of the library; such ancient topics may fall within her purview.
